chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَقْطَعُ الصَّلَاةَ مِنَ الضَّحِكِ وَ الْحَدَثِ وَ الْإِشَارَةِ وَ النِّسْيَانِ وَ غَيْرِ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 45 – What cuts off the Salāt, from the laugh, and the breaking of the ablution, and the gesture, and the forgetfulness, and other than that

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/240)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/240)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَلْتَفِتُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ قَالَ لَا وَ لَا يَنْقُضُ أَصَابِعَهُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Safwan, from Al A’ala, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws, said, ‘I asked himasws about the man who turns during the Salāt. Heasws said: ‘No (he cannot), nor can he break (crack) his fingers’.
en.sarwar: 12. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Safwan from al-‘Ala’ from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “I once asked abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, if a man in Salah (prayer) can turn around. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, and he must not crack his fingers also.’”
